## Spec Contradiction Detected

I've read both acceptance criteria carefully and they directly contradict each other:

| | Criterion | Expiry Window |
|---|---|---|
| **AC1** | Link is valid for 24 hours from time of issue | **24 hours** |
| **AC2** | Link expired when clicked more than 1 hour after issue | **1 hour** |

These cannot both be true. A token cannot simultaneously expire after 1 hour (AC2) and be valid for 24 hours (AC1).

---

## Why I'm Not Guessing

If I pick **24 hours** → AC2's test will fail (tokens between 1–24 hours would be accepted, not rejected).  
If I pick **1 hour** → AC1's test will fail (tokens described as "valid for 24 hours" would be rejected at 2 hours).

Either choice produces a broken implementation against one of the criteria. This is a **Bird-level domain question** — the correct expiry window is a business rule, not an implementation detail.

---

## What I Need

Please confirm with Bird/the domain authority:

> **What is the intended expiry window for password reset tokens?**
> - Option A: **24 hours** (AC1 is correct, AC2 has a typo)
> - Option B: **1 hour** (AC2 is correct, AC1 has a typo)
> - Option C: A different value entirely

Once that's resolved, I can implement `validateResetToken` in under 5 minutes. I'm ready to go the moment the spec is clean.
